signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.47 remains bid as the 289bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry advantage offsets geopolitical volatility from the Iranian President's resignation and IRGC takeover. While JPY COT LF net short -86,249 reflects a crowded carry trade, the 25d RR at -1.04 confirms the options market is paying a moderate premium for downside protection against BoJ intervention risk. Carry remains the dominant regime driver until VIX exceeds 25, though the 160.00 level is the critical threshold for potential BoJ verbal or physical intervention.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5970 faces a significant positioning mismatch. COT LF net short -13,767 (specs short) contradicts the RBNZ hawkish bias (hike priced for next meeting). This institutional short-squeeze risk is amplified by an extreme retail 86% short position. If the RBNZ delivers a hawkish hold or hike, the combined capitulation of retail and speculative shorts targets a move toward 0.6100. NZD/USD vs NZX 50 correlation at +0.412 (z=+1.78σ) suggests the pair is decoupling from local equities to follow rate differentials.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Manufacturing Miss
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7183 shows signs of late-trend exhaustion following the Australia Manufacturing PMI slip to 50.7. Despite a 60bp carry advantage over the USD, the options market is pricing extreme caution with an IV of 14.9% vs HV30 of 8.2% (VRP +6.7%). This elevated vol premium suggests traders are aggressively hedging downside risk rather than chasing the 1M +0.73% gain. Watch for a break below 0.7150 to confirm a shift in the bullish trend.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Retail Extreme + Real Rate Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7832 is testing retail sentiment limits with 85% of community traders long. This extreme retail long position diverges from the institutional COT net short -5,651, creating a contrarian bearish signal. While the nominal Fed-SNB spread is +375bp, the real rate differential is narrower (+1.22% USD vs -0.31% CHF), suggesting the carry trade is less restrictive than nominals imply. Retail capitulation risk rises if price breaks the 1M low near 0.7800.
